citation.description: outbound_differ.py is in _ZERO_ADAPTER_IMPORT_SCALAR_CORE tuple; test_scalar_core_imports_no_jira_adapter walks the full AST and asserts no adapters.jira import. Confirmed by search: no 'adapters.jira' string in outbound_differ.py.

criteria.criterion: `outbound_differ.py` contains NO `adapters.jira` import (module-level or function-nested) — verified by the extended AST case in `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_backend_neutrality.py`.

citation.description: _VENDOR_SNAPSHOT_KEY_LITERALS denylist and test_outbound_differ_names_no_vendor_snapshot_key defined. Runtime spy test at test_canonical_outbound_equivalence.py:140-174. Implementation in outbound_field_diff.py:187 calls inbound_mapper.map_remote_to_local(jira_fields) before any comparison.

criteria.criterion: The update path canonicalizes the snapshot via the injected `InboundMapper` before any comparison (runtime proof: spy InboundMapper observes snapshot entry), and `outbound_differ.py` contains none of the vendor payload key literals `"accountId"`, `"emailAddress"`, `"displayName"`, `"issuetype"`, `"issuelinks"`, `"inwardIssue"`, `"outwardIssue"` — verified by a deterministic source-text denylist case in `test_backend_neutrality.py`.

citation.description: Canonical-comparison corollary block added under §(d) decision 2, stating core diffs in LOCAL shape, canonicalizes via InboundMapper, emits via OutboundMapper, baseline canonicalized at read time. Delivery note names ticket 625b for FIELD+BASELINE and eefd for LINK.

citation.description: Implementation is concrete and non-stub: outbound_field_diff.py provides compute_update_fields and diff_canonical_fields; inbound_fields.py has presence-guarded partial-tolerant mapping; adapters/jira/backend.py has map_fields_to_remote and resolve_assignee; test files are non-stub with concrete assertions. citation.description: _map_jira_to_local_fields guards every field emission on `if key in jira_fields` (presence, not truthiness). Heldout test test_map_remote_to_local_partial_omits_absent_keys verifies partial input omits absent keys and full input emits assignee_identity.

criteria.criterion: `map_remote_to_local` maps a partial `_BASELINE_FIELDS` subset by emitting ONLY the canonical keys whose vendor source key is PRESENT (absent vendor key -> absent canonical key, never a default), while a FULL snapshot maps byte-identically to today — verified by a partial-input test and a full-input equivalence test.

citation.description: test_field_absent_from_baseline_is_no_ancestor_local_wins: baseline carries only summary (no status), status has no ancestor, local-wins emits {status: Done}. Implementation in outbound_field_diff.py:251 checks `field not in baseline` to skip suppression.

criteria.criterion: A field absent from the canonicalized baseline is treated as 'no ancestor' (per-field local-wins), matching today's whole-`None`-baseline arbitration — verified by a characterization case in the equivalence suite.
